Subject: Robin Hood: Men in Tights (1993)
Description: The Sherwood Forest archer (Cary Elwes) leads his melting pot of outlaws against Prince John (Richard Lewis) and the Sheriff of Rottingham (Roger Rees).

Subject: Spaceballs (1987)
Description: President Skroob (Mel Brooks) pits evil Dark Helmet (Rick Moranis) against Lone Starr and the half-man, half-dog Barf (John Candy).

Subject: Planet Earth: Frozen Planet - Spring
Description: Spring in the polar regions; the greatest seasonal transformation of the planet begins with the return of the sun.

Subject: Planet Earth: Frozen Planet - Summer
Description: It is high summer in the polar regions, and the sun never sets; many species take advantage of the easier conditions, but summer is a tough time for the polar bear family, as their ice world melts away and the cubs take their first swimming lesson.

Subject: Seven Worlds, One Planet - Antarctica
Description: Icy Antarctica is a land of survivors enduring the most hostile conditions on Earth.

Subject: Planet Earth: Dynasties - Emperor Penguins
Description: A colony of emperor penguins arrive in Atka Bay, after three months at sea, to raise their chicks through the coming winter.

Subject: Planet Earth: The Blue Planet - Frozen Seas
Description: Life on the edge of frozen seas near the North and South poles.

Subject: Planet Earth: Frozen Planet - Winter
Description: Winter is the greatest test for life in the polar regions; in the Arctic, a female polar bear treks into the mountains to give birth; out on the frozen ocean, the entire population of the world's spectacled eider ducks braves the winter.

Subject: Planet Earth - Ice Worlds
Description: New science about the dynamics of emperor penguin behavior and aerial images of a polar bear swimming.

Subject: Planet Earth: Frozen Planet II - Frozen Lands
Description: Going back to the far North of the planet where the largest landhabitat on Earth exists, home to great Boreal forests and the barren tundra.

Subject: Planet Earth: Frozen Planet II - Frozen Worlds
Description: After being raised on the ice in winter, emperor penguin chicks find themselves abandoned by their parents in spring; to survive, they must find their own way across the treacherous sea ice to the rich waters of the Southern Ocean.

Subject: Planet Earth: Frozen Planet II - Frozen Peaks
Description: Found on every continent, mountains create unique frozen worlds, home to extraordinary animals that must overcome the crushing conditions of the cold.

Subject: Rudy (1993)
Description: With heart and determination an Illinois youth (Sean Astin) tackles shortcomings to play Notre Dame football.

Subject: Field of Dreams (1989)
Description: An inspired Iowa farmer builds a baseball field, then sees ``Shoeless`` Joe Jackson's ghost and other marvels.

Subject: Jerry Maguire (1996)
Description: A Los Angeles sports agent (Tom Cruise) finds love with a pretty accountant (Renee Zellweger) after an attack of conscience costs him his job and fiancee.

Subject: A Few Good Men (1992)
Description: Navy lawyers (Tom Cruise, Demi Moore) defend two Marines accused of killing a private at the naval station at Guantanamo Bay, Cuba.

Subject: Law & Order - Gunplay
Description: An illegal firearms sting backfires when two officers are slain, and Green poses as a gun buyer to try to capture the killers, who were able to identify the officers via a Web site identifying undercover cops.

Subject: Law & Order - Cut
Description: McCoy must determine whether charges should be filed in civil court or in criminal court when a novelist's death seems to have been caused by careless liposuction, but Branch finds a recurring pattern of negligence by the doctor.

Subject: Law & Order - Gov Love
Description: McCoy takes the issue of gay marriage before the state's Supreme Court when he is unable to compel a man to testify against a crooked developer because the two say they are married.

Subject: Law & Order - Cry Wolf
Description: The detectives proceed with skeptical caution when a radio personality, known to stage fake muggings for publicity, claims to have been shot and almost killed.

Subject: Law & Order - All in the Family
Description: A philandering jeweler with ties to the Russian mob is one of four people shot to death on a sidewalk, and Fontana and Green learn the victim was cooperating with federal authorities until his murder.

Subject: David Attenborough's Global Adventure: The Rise of Nature
David Attenborough's Global Adventure: Home Planet
Description: From the idyllic shores of the Galapagos to the underwater worlds of the Great Barrier Reef, David Attenborough seeks to understand how the natural world has thrived in what might otherwise seem like impossible environments.

Subject: David Attenborough's Global Adventure
Description: In his eighties, David Attenborough embarked on a seven-year journey; from the stunning Great Barrier Reef to the rainforests of Borneo, follow his voyage across the globe.

Subject: Rudy (1993)
Description: With heart and determination an Illinois youth (Sean Astin) tackles shortcomings to play Notre Dame football.

Subject: Field of Dreams (1989)
Description: An inspired Iowa farmer builds a baseball field, then sees ``Shoeless`` Joe Jackson's ghost and other marvels.

Subject: Jerry Maguire (1996)
Description: A Los Angeles sports agent (Tom Cruise) finds love with a pretty accountant (Renee Zellweger) after an attack of conscience costs him his job and fiancee.

Subject: A Few Good Men (1992)
Description: Navy lawyers (Tom Cruise, Demi Moore) defend two Marines accused of killing a private at the naval station at Guantanamo Bay, Cuba.

Subject: Hacksaw Ridge (2016)
Description: During World War II's Battle of Okinawa, U.S. Army medic Desmond Doss (Andrew Garfield) braves bullets, grenades and snipers while single-handedly evacuating the wounded from behind enemy lines.

Subject: Law & Order - Mammon
Description: When a wealthy venture-capitalist is found dead in his home, Fontana and Green zero in on the victim's young wife (Andrea Roth) and her contractor boyfriend (Daniel Sunjata).

Subject: Law & Order - Ain't No Love
Description: Evidence in the shooting death of a rap legend seems to point to his young protege (Sean Nelson), who has been cutting his own music on bootleg CDs, so Fontana and Green listen for clues in the lyrics.

Subject: Law & Order - Fluency
Description: When nine flu victims suddenly die, Green and Fontana learn that all had been injected with a fake vaccine, which leads to the arrest and prosecution of a longtime con man.

Subject: Law & Order - Obsession
Description: When a controversial conservative talk-show host is shot to death, detectives sift through many possible suspects, eventually focusing on the man's widow and a woman who may have been stalking her.

Subject: Law & Order - The Sixth Man
Description: A man strangled in his rent-controlled apartment leads detectives to the building's owner, who wants to convert the building to a co-op, but a basketball player's fingerprints are found at the scene.

Subject: Law & Order - License to Kill
Description: McCoy and Borgia target the unlikely hero who pursued a mass murderer through the streets of Manhattan in a deadly car chase that left one dead and one injured.

Subject: Law & Order - Dining Out
Description: A celebrity chef comes under suspicion in the murder of a TV network executive when Fontana and Green learn that the two were having an affair and the chef's cooking show was about to be canceled.

Subject: The Three Stooges - Woman Haters
Description: The boys, as three traveling salesmen, vow never to wed, but unbeknownst to Moe and Curly, Larry has recently been married.

Subject: You Nazty Spy! (1940)
Description: The Three Stooges gain control of Moronica.

Subject: The Three Stooges - Half-Wits Holiday
Description: A professor bets he can turn the boys into gentlemen.

Subject: Bones - The Gunk in the Garage
Description: After an explosion in a hotel garage, the Jeffersonian team tries to identify victims by investigating remains found at the scene; Sweets works with a temporary partner (Danielle Panabaker) while Booth works on the department's budget.

Subject: Bones - The Tiger in the Tale
Description: While investigating the death of an animal expo employee, the team is drawn into the world of animal trafficking; Brennan thinks she would be a good presidential candidate; Sweets and Daisy make a change.

Subject: Bones - The Method in the Madness
Description: The team investigates the death of a woman whose body was found in the trash; Booth invites Sweets to stay with him and Brennan until he finds a new place.

Subject: Bones - The Patriot in Purgatory
Description: While examining bodies deemed unidentifiable, interns uncover the identity of a man who died in the 9/11 terrorist attacks.

Subject: The Addams Family (1991)
Description: When a man claiming to be Fester, the missing brother of Gomez Addams, arrives at the Addams' home, the family is thrilled. Morticia begins to suspect the man is a fraud, since he cannot recall details of Fester's life.

Subject: Addams Family Values (1993)
Description: Upon arrival of their mustachioed baby, Morticia and Gomez hire a greedy nanny who targets Uncle Fester.

Subject: Hook (1991)
Description: When Captain James Hook kidnaps his children, an adult Peter Pan must return to Neverland and reclaim his youthful spirit in order to challenge his old enemy.

Subject: A Knight's Tale (2001)
Description: An English commoner (Heath Ledger) dons the armor of a dead jouster and, with the help of friends (Mark Addy), competes against nobles in 14th-century France.

Subject: The Princess Bride (1987)
Description: A storybook stableboy turns pirate (Cary Elwes) and rescues his beloved (Robin Wright), who is about to marry a dreadful prince.

Subject: Law & Order - Sects
Description: While investigating a woman's murder, Fontana and Green uncover a cult that encourages sexual relations with children, and Borgia determines to put away its mesmerizing leader (Deborah Hedwall).

Subject: Law & Order - Tombstone
Description: A promiscuous lawyer ends up bludgeoned to death in her office, and detectives treat her former lovers as suspects until DNA evidence points to a co-worker.

Subject: Law & Order - Publish and Perish
Description: Fontana and Falco (Michael Imperioli) must tread lightly when they discover links between a powerful police commissioner and the murders of an infamous porn actress and a maverick publisher.

Subject: Law & Order - Sport of Kings
Description: The shooting of a Panamanian jockey sends Fontana and Falco into the colorful world of horse racing; they learn that the animal's owner used stolen funds for the purchase.

Subject: Law & Order - In God We Trust
Description: Fontana and Falco make an arrest after a blaze kills a firefighter; the discovery of a charred pistol leads them to reopen the investigation into an old murder.

Subject: Law & Order - Locomotion
Description: A commuter train strikes an SUV parked on the tracks, leaving 11 dead; Fontana and Falco sift through the wreckage in search of clues until they find a depressed construction worker who claims he meant to commit suicide, not homicide.

Subject: Law & Order - Red Ball
Description: McCoy and Borgia consider making a deal with an ex-convict in order to save an abducted five-year-old girl.

Subject: Hidden Habitats - Amazon
Description: The diversity of the Amazon and the strategies used by the jungle life to make the most of limited nutrients.

Subject: Seven Worlds, One Planet - Australia
Description: Isolated for millions of years, Australia has a variety of unique animals, including the most dangerous bird in the world, kangaroos, wombats, koalas and predators.

Subject: The Addams Family (1991)
Description: When a man claiming to be Fester, the missing brother of Gomez Addams, arrives at the Addams' home, the family is thrilled. Morticia begins to suspect the man is a fraud, since he cannot recall details of Fester's life.

Subject: The Princess Bride (1987)
Description: A storybook stableboy turns pirate (Cary Elwes) and rescues his beloved (Robin Wright), who is about to marry a dreadful prince.

Subject: The Three Musketeers (1993)
Description: Swordsmen Aramis (Charlie Sheen), Athos (Kiefer Sutherland), Porthos and D'Artagnan (Chris O'Donnell) foil Cardinal Richelieu's plot to assassinate the king.

Subject: A Knight's Tale (2001)
Description: An English commoner (Heath Ledger) dons the armor of a dead jouster and, with the help of friends (Mark Addy), competes against nobles in 14th-century France.

Subject: Space Cowboys (2000)
Description: A group of pilots need to come and serve their country and fulfill their dream of going to space. Four aging astronauts who never made it into space agree to go up and repair a 1950s satellite.

Subject: Point Break (1991)
Description: An FBI agent (Keanu Reeves) turns California surfer to nab bank robbers who wear rubber masks of four ex-presidents.

Subject: Criminal Minds - Natural Born Killer
Description: The Behavioral Analysis Unit believes a serial killer is responsible when an undercover police officer investigating the mob is taken captive.

Subject: Criminal Minds - Derailed
Description: Hotchner and Gideon race to save Elle and her fellow passengers when a paranoid schizophrenic man has a delusional episode on a train and takes the other riders hostage.

Subject: Criminal Minds - The Popular Kids
Description: When two high-school students are murdered in a small town, Hotchner and his team suspect they may have been the victims of a cult killing.

Subject: Criminal Minds - Blood Hungry
Description: Hotchner and his team attempt to get inside the mind of a psychotic killer whose fantasies lead him to murder.

Subject: Criminal Minds - What Fresh Hell?
Description: The alibi of an abducted girl's father clears him of suspicion, leaving the profilers to search her seemingly safe neighborhood for the person who is responsible.

Subject: Criminal Minds - Poison
Description: The team tries to determine who is poisoning residents of a small town.

Subject: Criminal Minds - Riding the Lightning
Description: Gideon becomes convinced of a woman's innocence when the team interviews husband-and-wife serial killers just days before their scheduled executions.

Subject: Planet Earth: Life - Fish
Description: The secret and ferocious world of the great white shark, sailfish, puffer fish and barracuda.

Subject: South Pacific - Ocean of Islands
Description: A closer look into the blue will reveal a scattering of islands, from the Tropics to the sub-Antartic waters of the Southern Ocean.

Subject: South Pacific - Castaways
Description: The ultimate castaways, from saltwater crocodiles and giant eels to crested iguanas, overcome great odds to reach islands thousands of miles apart.

Subject: South Pacific - Fragile Paradise
Description: A fragile paradise in the South Pacific is revealed; international fishing fleets take a serious toll on the region's sharks, albatross and tuna.

Subject: South Pacific - Ocean of Volcanoes
Description: The violent eruption of underwater volcanoes produce coral reefs that support large groups of grey reef sharks and giant manta rays; strange creatures including the vampire bug and megapode -- a bird that uses volcanic springs to incubate its eggs.

Subject: South Pacific - Endless Blue
Description: A large part of the remote, blue wilderness of the South Pacific is a marine desert, in which many animals go to extraordinary lengths to survive.

Subject: South Pacific - Strange Islands
Description: With flightless parrots, burrowing bats, giant skinks and kangaroos in trees, wildlife has evolved in extraordinary ways on the isolated islands of the South Pacific.

Subject: Cape Fear (1991)
Description: A tattooed psychopath (Robert De Niro) preys on a Southern lawyer (Nick Nolte), his wife (Jessica Lange) and their teenage daughter.

Subject: Edward Scissorhands (1990)
Description: The solitary life of an artificial man - who was incompletely constructed and has scissors for hands - is upended when he is taken in by a suburban family.

Subject: Sleepy Hollow (1999)
Description: Constable Ichabod Crane is sent to the village of Sleepy Hollow, where a headless horseman is leaving a trail of decapitated bodies.

Subject: The Lost Boys (1987)
Description: A woman and her sons move to a coastal California town for a fresh start, unaware that it is full of teenage vampire punks.

Subject: The Graham Norton Show - New Year's Eve Show
NCIS - Sub Rosa
Description: A decomposed body found in a tub of acid at a naval base leads Gibbs to deduce that an impostor is serving aboard one of the nuclear submarines.

Subject: NCIS - Minimum Security
Description: The team travels to Guantanamo Bay in Cuba to probe a Navy translator's death caused by swallowing emeralds smuggled from Afghanistan.

Subject: Yes Man (2008)
Description: After attending a self-help seminar, a negative-thinking man brings about a change in his life by saying yes to everything.

Subject: Galaxy Quest (1999)
Description: Aliens, believing actors to be real heroes, enlist cast members of a sci-fi TV series to help save their people.

Subject: College Football - Pittsburgh at Florida State
Description: The Panthers (3-2, 1-1 ACC) face the No. 25 Seminoles (3-2, 0-2). Mason Heintschel threw for 323 yards and four TDs in his first career start Oct. 4 as Pittsburgh blasted Boston College 48-7. Florida State comes off a 28-22 loss to then-No. 3 Miami.

Subject: College Football - Clemson at South Carolina
Description: The Gamecocks (4-7) face the Tigers (6-5) in a Palmetto Series contest. South Carolina claimed two of the last three matchups, but Clemson has won five consecutive trips to Columbia, S.C. The Tigers have won three in a row to secure a bowl game.

Subject: College Football - Georgia Tech at Boston College
Description: The No. 16 Yellow Jackets (8-1, 5-1) refocus on the ACC title game as they play the Eagles (1-9, 0-6). Entering Week 12, Georgia Tech sat in a four-game tie for first with Virginia, Pittsburgh and SMU after a 48-36 upset loss at NC State on Nov. 1.

Subject: College Football - Boca Raton Bowl: Toledo vs. Louisville
Description: The Cardinals (8-4) line up against the Rockets (8-4) in the Boca Raton Bowl. Toledo won bowl games in two of the last three years, moving to 2-0 at the Boca Raton Bowl by beating Liberty in 2022. The Rockets are riding a four-game win streak.

Subject: ESPNU Instant Classic - Florida State at Virginia
College Football - Union Home Mortgage Gasparilla Bowl: Memphis vs. NC State
Description: The Tigers and Wolfpack get together for the Gasparilla Bowl. The programs have never met. Memphis has won four bowl games in a row, while NC State has lost five straight. The Wolfpack come in hot following a 42-19 thumping of the rival Tar Heels.

Description: North Carolina hosts East Carolina at the Dean E. Smith Center. Star Caleb Wilson (19.5 PPG) leads the Tar Heels against a high scoring Pirates squad. Guard Jordan Riley (21.7 PPG) looks to lead his team in this intense non-conference match.

Description: The No. 7 Aggies host the No. 10 Hurricanes in the College Football Playoff's first round. Texas A&M saw its undefeated season, and its chance at the SEC title, spoiled in a Nov. 28 loss at Texas. These programs traded home wins in 2022 and 2023.

Subject: Women's College Basketball - Wofford at Georgia Tech
Description: The Yellow Jackets (4-9,4-2) host the Terriers (5-7,2-5) at home for a post holiday match. After a three game skid, the Jackets and guard Talayah Walker look to defend home court against forward Queen Ikhiuwu who averages 15.1 points per game.

Subject: Women's College Basketball - Stetson at Miami
Description: The Hurricanes host the Hatters following a dominant victory over Kennesaw State. Center Ra Shaya Kyle anchors the paint after a double-double performance. The visitors travel to Coral Gables seeking momentum after a narrow win over Stonehill.

Subject: Women's College Basketball - Duke at Syracuse
Description: The Orange host the Blue Devils in Syracuse. Forward Uche Izoje averages 8.2 rebounds per game following a decisive win over Mercyhurst. Forward Toby Fournier leads the visitors with 7.4 rebounds per game as both teams battle for conference position.

Description: Pittsburgh and Miami meet in Coral Gables for their ACC opener. The 7-6 Panthers look to extend a two-game win streak, while the 11-2 Hurricanes seek their seventh straight victory. Cameron Corhen leads the Pitt attack in this conference clash.

Subject: College Basketball - Louisville at California
Description: No. 16 Louisville and California open ACC play in Berkeley. The Bears are 12-1 and seek a 10th straight win, while the 10-2 Cardinals look to Ryan Conwell to lead the offense. Mikel Brown Jr. is expected to return to the floor for Louisville.

Subject: Women's College Basketball - Penn State at Iowa

Description: Classic golf match between two time Masters and PGA Championship winner Byron Nelson and Gene Littler, who was the defending U.S. Open Champion at the time of the match. From Pine Valley Golf Club in Pine Valley, N.J.

Masters Highlights - 1997
Description: At age 21, Tiger Woods became the youngest champion in Masters history. Woods breaks the 72-hole record by one stroke, scoring a four-day total 18-under-par 270. His 12-stroke margin of victory over runner-up Tom Kite also sets a tournament record.

Subject: PGA Championship Highlights - 1999: Tiger Woods
Description: The 1999 PGA Championship at Medinah Country Club was the setting where Tiger Woods earned his second major title with a par on the 72nd hole. His final round 72 was just enough to edge runner-up, Spain's Sergio Garcia.

Subject: U.S. Open Golf Highlights - 2000
Description: Tiger Woods dominates Pebble Beach and the field to win his first U.S. Open title. His fifteen stroke margin of victory was the largest in major tournament history.

Subject: The Open Highlights - 2000: The Open
Description: Tiger Woods wins.

Subject: PGA Championship Highlights - Tiger Woods at Valhalla Golf Club
Description: Tiger Woods earned his second straight PGA Championship in an epic 3-hole playoff battle with Bob May at Valhalla Golf Club,in Louisville, Ky.

Subject: Masters Highlights - 2001
Description: Winning an unprecedented fourth consecutive major title, Tiger Woods captures the 2001 Masters Tournament with a 16-under-par total 272. Woods defeats runners-up David Duval (274) and Phil Mickelson (275).

Subject: Masters Highlights - 2002
Description: Tiger Woods plays steady, and sometimes spectacular, golf to capture his third Masters. He joins Jack Nicklaus (1965-66) and Nick Faldo (1989-90) as the only players to successfully defend their title.

Subject: U.S. Open Golf Highlights - 2002: Tiger Woods at Bethpage
Description: Tiger Woods continued his trek towards the Grand Slam by winning the 2002 U.S. Open at Bethpage State Park in N.Y. and becomes the first golfer to win the first two legs of the Grand Slam in the same year since Jack Nicklaus in 1972.

Subject: Masters Highlights - 2005
Description: Tiger Woods scores a memorable chip-in at No. 16, then defeats Chris DiMarco with a 15-foot birdie putt on the first playoff hole to became the third person to win at least four Masters tournaments, joining Jack Nicklaus (6) and Arnold Palmer (4).

Subject: The Open Highlights - 2005: Tiger Woods at St. Andrews
Description: Highlights of Tiger Woods' performance at the 2005 Open Championship, held at St Andrews, Scotland.

Subject: The Open Highlights - 2006: Tiger Woods at Royal Liverpool
Description: Tiger Woods wins.

Subject: PGA Championship Highlights - 2006
Description: Tiger Woods returns to rare form with a five stroke victory over Shaun Micheel at the 88th PGA Championship at Medinah Country Club. The win marks Woods' 3rd PGA Championship and the 12th major of his career.

Subject: PGA Championship Highlights - 2007
Description: Defending champion Tiger Woods wins his fourth PGA Championship and 13th major title, two strokes ahead of runner-up Woody Austin. From Southern Hills Country Club in Tulsa, Okla.

Subject: U.S. Open Golf Highlights - 2008: Tiger Woods at Torrey Pines
Description: In what has to be one of the greatest U.S. Open tournaments in history, Rocco Mediate takes Tiger Woods to the limit in an epic 18-hole playoff. Even after 18 extra holes, both men were still tied and went to sudden death where Tiger Woods prevailed.

Subject: Masters Highlights - 2019 - Tiger Woods
Description: In one of the most emotional finishes in tournament history, Tiger Woods came from behind to win his fifth green jacket by a shot, firing 2-under-par (70) and finishing at 13-under (275). It had been 14 years since he last won the Masters (2005).

Subject: Decade of Dominance
Description: A commemorative look at the 10-year anniversary of the College Football Playoff that highlights the SEC's success across the last decade. During that time, the SEC earned 12 CFP appearances and six National Championships -- the most of any league.

Subject: SEC Inside - Football Championship
Description: Each week, a half-hour episode will focus on a select team and explore previously unaired footage and sounds from game preparation, the sideline and following the final whistle.

Subject: Southern Hoops: A History of SEC Basketball - Part One (1930-1959)
Description: Adolph Rupp comes to the University of Kentucky in 1930, two years before the SEC is formed, and his Wildcats go on to dominate play in the conference for decades. Meanwhile, a future coaching legend later known as Pat Summitt is born in Tennessee.

Subject: Southern Hoops: A History of SEC Basketball - Part Two (1960-1970)
Description: Texas Western's historic win over Adolph Rupp's Kentucky team in the 1966 NCAA final marks a turning point in the SEC. Vanderbilt's Perry Wallace becomes the conference's first Black player, while at LSU, Pete Maravich rewrites the record books.

Subject: Southern Hoops: A History of SEC Basketball - Part Three (1971-1979)
Description: Dale Brown brings an infectious personality to LSU while Pat Summitt begins her legendary coaching career at Tennessee. And the year after Adolph Rupp's death in 1977, his successor Joe B. Hall leads Kentucky to its first NCAA title in 20 years.

Subject: Southern Hoops: A History of SEC Basketball - Part Four (1980-1989)
Description: Georgia's Dominique Wilkins and Auburn's Charles Barkley headline the SEC's successes in the early 1980s, while LSU's Dale Brown takes two teams to the Final Four. Pat Summitt wins her first two national titles at Tennessee.

Subject: Southern Hoops: A History of SEC Basketball - Part Five (1990-1998)
Description: Arkansas joins the SEC and becomes one of the conference's top teams, led by fiery coach Nolan Richardson and his ``40 Minutes of Hell'' attack. Kentucky returns to prominence, and at Tennessee, Pat Summitt wins three straight NCAA titles.

Subject: Southern Hoops: A History of SEC Basketball - Part Six (1998-2010)
Description: In his first year at Kentucky, Tubby Smith becomes the third Black coach ever to win a national title. Florida's back-to-back NCAA crowns mark the rise of a new power. The Lady Vols win 2 straight NCAA titles as LSU reaches 5 straight Final Fours.

Subject: Southern Hoops: A History of SEC Basketball - Part Seven (2011-2022)
Description: John Calipari continues the winning tradition at Kentucky with a national championship in 2012. Four years later, Pat Summitt passes away after battling early-onset Alzheimer's disease. Dawn Staley leads South Carolina to a pair of NCAA titles.

Subject: Women's College Basketball - Providence at South Carolina
Description: The Gamecocks host the Friars in Columbia. Gamecocks forward Joyce Edwards averages 22.0 points per game following a dominant win over FGCU. Guard Sabou Gueye leads the visiting attack as both teams battle for non-conference momentum.

Subject: Women's College Basketball - Southeastern Louisiana at Texas
Description: The No. 2 Longhorns Host the Lady Lions at the Moody Center. Forward Madison Booker averages 18.7 points per game to lead a potent offense. Guard Aliyah Collins fuels the visiting attack with 13.6 points per game as both teams return to action.

Subject: Women's College Basketball - Alabama State at LSU
Description: No. 5 LSU (13-0) hosts Alabama State (3-7) at the Pete Maravich Assembly Center. The undefeated Tigers seek to extend their win streak, while junior forward Charlese McClendon, averaging 7.5 points, leads the Lady Hornets' upset bid againt LSU.
